headline: The Dragons Went Full Cobra Kai on the Benchwarmers and I'm Not Sure They're Legally Allowed to Do That
subheadline: A 9-0 beatdown, the Twins looking scary good, and yes, we need to talk about someone named their team the Buttville Skidmarks.
body:
We need to start with the **Athletic Park Dragons** going full scorched-earth, no-mercy, sweep-the-leg on the **Benchwarmers** with a 9.0-0.0 annihilation. Nine to ZERO. That's not a fantasy baseball score, that's what happens when you accidentally leave your lineup empty because you were binge-watching Severance and forgot what day it was. (We've all been there. We've ALL been there.) The Dragons didn't just win this matchup—they made the Benchwarmers question their life choices. This was the fantasy equivalent of the Red Wedding, except somehow more violent.

Meanwhile, the **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** absolutely demolished **North Correa** 8.0-1.0, and look, I'm officially putting the league on notice: this team might be a problem. They're giving off serious 2017 Warriors vibes right now—the kind of team where you check the score, see you're down 6-0 in three categories, and just... close the app. Walk away. Maybe take up gardening. The Twins are currently sitting atop the standings (more on that weirdness in a second), and they're playing like a team that actually drafted with a plan instead of just panic-picking whoever Yahoo had ranked highest.

I also need to pour one out for **BaseballPro77**, who lost to the **Central Wisconsin Curd Fryers** 6.0-4.0. First of all: incredible name. Curd Fryers. That's the kind of Wisconsin energy that makes me want to move to Madison and gain forty pounds. But BaseballPro77—buddy, you can't have a name that confident and then go .500 against deep-fried cheese. That's like calling yourself MovieExpert88 and then saying your favorite film is Morbius.

(Quick shoutout to **General Hospital** for squeaking past the **Sugar Land Skeeters** 5.0-4.0. That's a soap opera team beating a team named after a mosquito, which feels right somehow.)

Now, about those standings: everyone's showing 0-0 with zero points for, which either means we're still in preseason mode or our commissioner had one too many Miller Lites and forgot to update the database. Either way, I'm choosing to believe the Dragons are secretly 47-0 in alternate dimensions.

Next week: Can anyone slow down the Twins? Will the Benchwarmers recover from their traumatic experience? And seriously—**Buttville Skidmarks**? Someone's parents are SO disappointed right now.
